tests.label: Binary Explorer starts with empty scene + binary overview

steps.name: be_initial_empty
steps.text: The graph area should show an EMPTY SCENE prompt with seed buttons. The data panel should show binary overview. No nodes should be in the graph SVG area.

tests.label: Seed Namespaces adds only namespace nodes (no neighbors)

steps.name: be_seed_namespaces
steps.text: The graph should show 3-8 circular nodes spread across the canvas with visible labels. Nodes should not be crammed together. The scene counter should show a small number.

tests.label: Click node adds only that node (no neighbor explosion)

steps.name: be_click_node_no_explosion
steps.text: A node should be highlighted with a purple glow. Edges (colored lines) should connect to neighbor nodes. The data panel should show the selected node name and connection count.

tests.label: Right-click Expand 6 adds limited neighbors

steps.name: be_context_menu_expand
steps.text: More nodes should have appeared compared to before. Edges should be visible connecting the expanded node to its new neighbors. Scene counter should have increased.

tests.label: Chat question renders and gets LLM response

steps.name: be_chat_question
steps.text: The chat panel should show a user message bubble and an assistant response bubble with markdown-formatted text about binary features.

tests.label: Thumbs up/down buttons appear on assistant messages

steps.name: be_thumbs_visible
steps.text: Below an assistant message, there should be small triangle up/down buttons. One should be highlighted (green for up, red for down).

tests.label: SAVE SESSION and CLEAR CHAT buttons work

steps.name: be_save_clear_session
steps.text: The chat panel should show suggested queries (not message bubbles). The SAVE SESSION and CLEAR CHAT buttons should not be visible.

tests.label: CLEAR button resets to empty scene

steps.name: be_clear_scene
steps.text: The graph area should show an EMPTY SCENE prompt with seed buttons. The data panel should show binary overview. No nodes should be in the graph SVG area.

tests.label: Chat intent shows collapsible QuerySpec debug

steps.name: be_intent_queryspec
steps.text: The chat panel should show a UI Command response with a QuerySpec debug line showing the action and target. A collapsible details element should be present.

tests.label: Progress panel displays ingestion header

steps.script: const el = document.querySelector('[data-testid="ingest-progress"], [data-testid="progress-panel"], .ingest-progress, .progress-panel'); return el !== null && el.offsetParent !== null

steps.name: be_ingest_progress_panel_header

tests.label: Progress panel renders 6 ingestion stages

tests.label: Ingestion stage labels are present and non-empty

steps.name: be_ingest_stage_labels
steps.text: The ingestion progress panel should be visible showing pipeline stages with status icons (pending/running/done). A progress bar should be visible.

tests.label: SSE events update stage progress in real time
Progress bar percentage advances as stages complete

steps.script: const bar = document.querySelector('[data-testid="ingest-progress-bar"], .progress-bar, [role="progressbar"]'); const after = bar ? (parseFloat(bar.style.width) || parseFloat(bar.getAttribute('aria-valuenow')) || 0) : 0; return after >= window.__ingestProgressBefore

tests.label: New binary can be seeded in graph explorer

steps.name: be_ingest_new_binary_seed
steps.text: The graph should show 3-8 circular nodes spread across the canvas with visible labels. Nodes should not be crammed together. The scene counter should show a small number.

tests.label: New binary nodes can be clicked and expanded

steps.script: const nodes = document.querySelectorAll('g.nodes g, [data-testid^="graph-node"], .graph-node'); if(nodes.length > 0) nodes[0].dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', {bubbles: true}))
document.querySelectorAll('g.nodes g, [data-testid^="graph-node"], .graph-node')[0]?.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('contextmenu', {bubbles: true, clientX: 400, clientY: 300}))

steps.name: be_ingest_new_binary_click_expand
steps.text: A node should be highlighted with a purple glow. Edges (colored lines) should connect to neighbor nodes. The data panel should show the selected node name and connection count.

tests.label: Visual: empty scene shows seed buttons and data overview

steps.name: empty_scene
steps.text: The page shows a dark graph area with an EMPTY SCENE prompt containing seed buttons (Seed: Namespaces, Seed: Top 8 Hubs). Below the graph is a data panel showing DROID binary overview with namespace chips and feature counts. On the right is a chat panel with suggested queries. Are all these elements visible?

tests.label: Visual: seeded namespaces show 3-5 spread nodes with labels

steps.name: seeded
steps.text: The graph shows 3-5 circular nodes spread across the dark canvas (not crammed together). Each node should have a visible label (daemon, droid, tunnel). The nodes should be different sizes. Are nodes visible, spread out, and labeled?

tests.label: Visual: clicked node shows glow, edges to neighbors, and data panel

steps.name: clicked_with_edges
steps.text: One node should be highlighted (purple glow/ring). There should be visible colored edges (lines with arrowheads) connecting the selected node to its neighbors. Additional neighbor nodes should have appeared. The data panel at the bottom should show the selected node name, connections count, and clickable connection chips. Are the selected node highlighted, edges visible, and neighbors shown?

tests.label: Visual: minimap shows node dots and viewport rectangle

steps.name: minimap
steps.text: In the bottom-right corner of the graph area, there should be a small dark rectangle (minimap) containing colored dots representing nodes and a dashed purple rectangle showing the current viewport. Is the minimap visible with dots and a viewport rectangle?

tests.label: Visual: data panel summary shows connections as clickable chips

steps.name: data_panel_summary
steps.text: The data panel at the bottom should show: (1) node description text, (2) large stat numbers for connections/fields/states/confidence, (3) connection chips grouped by edge type (TRIGGERS, EMITS, PAYLOAD, CONTAINS) with cyan-colored clickable names, (4) a Bookmark to Memory button. Are all these elements visible and properly formatted?

tests.label: Double-click fits graph to viewport

steps.name: dblclick_fit
steps.text: All namespace nodes should be visible and centered in the viewport after double-click fit. No nodes should be off-screen.

tests.label: Drag node stays in new position

steps.name: drag_node
steps.text: A node should have moved from its original position. The dragged node should be displaced from the cluster.

tests.label: Rapid clicks don't crash the graph

steps.name: rapid_clicks
steps.text: Graph should still be rendering with nodes visible. No blank/empty graph. No error overlay.

tests.label: Node hover shows tooltip with tier and connections

steps.name: hover_tooltip
steps.text: A tooltip should appear near the hovered node showing: node name, node type, tier badge (T0/T1/T2), connection count, and CLICK TO ANALYZE text.

tests.label: Tier badges visible on nodes (T0 green, T1 blue, T2 amber)

steps.name: tier_badges
steps.text: Small colored dots should be visible at the top-right of each node: green for T0, blue for T1, amber for T2.

tests.label: Edge arrowheads point in correct direction

steps.name: arrowheads
steps.text: Edges should have small colored arrowheads at their endpoints. Arrowheads point FROM source TO target. Triggers edges are green, emits are orange, payload are blue.

tests.label: State machine nodes have dashed ring

steps.name: state_ring
steps.text: State machine nodes should have an extra dashed circular ring around them, distinguishing them from regular nodes.

tests.label: Cluster hulls with labels around grouped nodes

steps.name: hulls
steps.text: Dashed purple outlines (convex hulls) should enclose groups of nodes that belong to the same namespace. Hull labels (daemon, droid, schema etc) should be visible.

tests.label: New nodes animate in (radius grows from 0)

steps.name: entrance_done
steps.text: Nodes should have grown from small to full size. Compare with the early screenshot — nodes should be larger now.

tests.label: High-degree nodes are visually larger than leaf nodes

steps.name: node_sizes
steps.text: Hub nodes (with high connection counts shown as badges) should be noticeably larger circles than nodes with few connections. Namespace nodes should be the largest.

tests.label: Breadcrumb click navigates back to previous node

steps.name: breadcrumbs
steps.text: A breadcrumb trail should show 2+ node names separated by arrows. The last breadcrumb should be highlighted in accent color.

tests.label: Save scene and load it from dropdown

steps.name: scene_saved
steps.text: The scene name input should have been used and a scene saved. The saved scenes section should appear in the left pane.

tests.label: Right-click Remove from Scene removes the node

steps.name: removed_node
steps.text: One fewer node should be visible. The graph should have one less node than before the remove action.

tests.label: Stratified layout arranges nodes in horizontal tiers

steps.name: stratified
steps.text: Nodes should be arranged in horizontal tiers/layers based on type. Namespaces at top, RPCs in middle, events/schemas at bottom. NOT a random organic layout.

tests.label: Clustered layout groups nodes by namespace

steps.name: clustered
steps.text: Nodes should be grouped into spatial clusters based on their namespace. Daemon nodes in one area, droid in another. Clear visual separation between clusters.

tests.label: Security perspective shows only security-relevant node types

steps.name: security_perspective
steps.text: Graph should show only security-relevant node types (RPCs, schemas, events, namespaces). State machines and parameters should be hidden.

tests.label: Thumbs down + correction creates training pair in /memory

steps.text: I meant the daemon namespace not a random node

steps.name: correction_flow
steps.text: The chat should show: user message, assistant response with thumbs, then a correction message. The correction should have been submitted as a new message.

tests.label: Minimap shows colored dots and viewport rectangle

steps.name: minimap_visible
steps.text: Bottom-right of the graph should show a small dark minimap rectangle with colored dots (matching node colors) and a dashed purple viewport rectangle.

tests.label: Browser supports WebGL API

steps.name: webgl_browser_support
steps.text: The Binary Explorer page is loaded. The browser supports WebGL — the graph canvas area should be visible and rendering without any 'WebGL not supported' error message.

tests.label: Canvas 2D context available for graph rendering

steps.name: webgl_canvas_2d
steps.text: Namespace nodes are rendered on the graph. The SVG/canvas rendering shows crisp circular nodes with visible labels. No rendering artifacts or broken elements.

tests.label: Graph renders correctly with large node set

steps.name: webgl_large_graph
steps.text: Graph shows multiple nodes including the clicked node and its neighbors. Nodes are distributed across the canvas without overlap. Edges connecting nodes are clearly visible. Rendering is smooth with no graphical glitches.

tests.label: Node circles render with smooth anti-aliased edges

steps.name: webgl_node_quality
steps.text: Node circles should have smooth rounded edges without pixelation or jagged borders. Each node should display a clear label text. The visual quality should appear crisp and professional.

tests.label: Edges render as smooth directional lines with arrowheads

steps.script: return document.querySelectorAll('g.edges line, g.edges path, line[class*=edge], path[class*=edge], .link').length >= 0

steps.name: webgl_edge_render
steps.text: After clicking a node, edges should be visible connecting the selected node to its neighbors. Edge lines should be smooth and thin. Arrowheads or directional indicators should be visible at edge endpoints.

tests.label: Graph rendering quality maintained during zoom/pan

steps.name: webgl_zoom_quality
steps.text: After zooming in on the graph, nodes should appear larger and still render with crisp edges and readable labels. The rendering quality should not degrade with zoom. No pixelation or blurriness should be visible.

tests.label: JOURNAL tab button is visible in right panel header

steps.name: journal_tab_exists
steps.text: The right panel header should show two tab buttons: ANALYSIS and JOURNAL. The JOURNAL tab should be visible and clickable. The tabs should appear at the top of the right panel.

tests.label: Clicking JOURNAL tab shows Investigation Journal panel

steps.name: journal_tab_switch
steps.text: After clicking the JOURNAL tab, the right panel should now show the 'Investigation Journal' heading with an empty timeline or placeholder text. The ANALYSIS chat should be hidden. The journal panel should be cleanly rendered.

tests.label: Clicking a node adds a step to the investigation journal

steps.name: journal_node_click
steps.text: The Investigation Journal should show at least one recorded step after the node was clicked. The step should show a timestamp, a node click icon/label, and a description mentioning the clicked node name. The journal timeline should display the entry clearly.

tests.label: Changing layout mode adds a step to the investigation journal

steps.name: journal_layout_change
steps.text: The Investigation Journal should show at least one step recorded for the layout change action. The step entry should mention 'Layout' or 'STRATIFIED'. The timeline should display chronologically ordered steps with timestamps.

tests.label: Journal badge shows step count after interactions

steps.name: journal_badge_count
steps.text: The JOURNAL tab button should display a small badge/pill with a number indicating how many steps have been recorded. After clicking 2 nodes, the badge should show at least 2. The badge should be prominently visible on the JOURNAL tab button.

tests.label: Changing perspective adds a step to the investigation journal

steps.name: journal_perspective
steps.text: The Investigation Journal should show a step entry for the perspective change action. The entry should mention 'Perspective' or 'Security'. Steps should be shown with timestamps in a timeline format.

tests.label: Expanding a node adds an expand step to the investigation journal

steps.name: journal_expand_step
steps.text: The Investigation Journal should show an expand step entry. The step should display an expand icon or label, the name of the node that was expanded, and a timestamp. The journal timeline should clearly distinguish the expand step from node-click steps.

tests.label: Asking a chat question adds a step to the investigation journal

steps.script: const inp=document.querySelector('input[placeholder*="Ask"],textarea[placeholder*="Ask"],input[type="text"]'); if(inp){inp.value='What does the daemon namespace do?'; inp.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true}));}

steps.name: journal_chat_question
steps.text: The Investigation Journal should show a chat/question step entry after the user asked a question. The step should display a chat bubble icon or Q&A label, the question text 'What does the daemon namespace do?', and a timestamp. The entry should be visually distinct from node-click steps.

tests.label: Clicking a journal step replays the scene state at that point

steps.name: journal_step_replay
steps.text: After clicking the first journal step entry, the graph scene should replay and show the state as it was at that point in the investigation. The replayed step should be highlighted in the journal timeline. The graph should reflect the historical state (fewer nodes than the current state).

tests.label: Deleting a journal step removes it from the timeline

steps.name: journal_delete_step
steps.text: After clicking the delete button on a journal step entry, that step should be removed from the investigation journal timeline. The remaining steps (if any) should still display correctly. The deleted step should no longer appear in the timeline.

tests.label: Adding a note to a journal step persists the annotation

steps.script: const noteInput=document.querySelector('[data-journal-note-input],textarea[placeholder*="note" i],input[placeholder*="note" i]'); if(noteInput){noteInput.value='Interesting entry point for privilege escalation'; noteInput.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true}));}

steps.name: journal_add_note
steps.text: After adding a note to a journal step, the step entry in the timeline should display the annotation text 'Interesting entry point for privilege escalation'. The note should appear below or alongside the step description. The note text should be clearly readable in the journal UI.

tests.label: Export journal as markdown produces downloadable report

steps.name: journal_export_markdown
steps.text: After clicking the Export button, a download should be triggered or a markdown preview modal should appear. The exported content should contain the investigation journal steps as markdown with headings, timestamps, and node names. An export success indicator, download prompt, or preview modal should be visible.

tests.label: UNDO button is visible in the graph toolbar

steps.name: undo_button_visible
steps.text: The graph toolbar should show UNDO (↩ UNDO) and REDO (↪ REDO) buttons. Both buttons should be visible in the toolbar area above or near the graph. Initially on an empty scene, the UNDO button should appear disabled/muted.

tests.label: REDO button is visible in the graph toolbar
UNDO button is disabled when scene is empty (no history)

steps.name: undo_disabled_empty
steps.text: On an empty scene with no actions taken, the UNDO button should appear greyed out / disabled with no active highlight. The UNDO button should have muted styling indicating it cannot be clicked. The REDO button should also be disabled.

tests.label: Undo after seeding removes nodes and restores empty scene

steps.name: undo_after_seed
steps.text: After pressing UNDO following a namespace seed, the graph should revert to the previous state with fewer nodes or an empty scene. The EMPTY SCENE prompt may reappear. The undo operation should visibly change the graph.

tests.label: Redo restores nodes after an undo operation

steps.name: redo_restores_nodes
steps.text: After pressing UNDO and then REDO, the graph should be restored to the seeded state with namespace nodes visible. The redo operation should re-populate the graph with the previously undone nodes. Node count should match the original seeded state.

tests.label: Ctrl+Z keyboard shortcut triggers undo operation

steps.name: undo_keyboard
steps.text: After pressing Ctrl+Z, the graph should show a visible change - either nodes removed from the scene or an undo-related notification in the chat panel. The scene history should have been navigated backwards by one step.
